essence: David built and validated an AI-generated templated start.sh system (simple/complex templates, s/s1/s2 aliases echoing underlying commands) unifying startup across the voice-agent and SupportSignal multi-service projects, then hit an unrelated iTerm Cmd+Shift+D split-terminal regression.

actions.text: Investigate why iTerm Cmd+Shift+D stopped splitting terminals

essence: David finalized the alias design: a single 's' alias delegating all logic to a per-project start.sh generated from a standard patch template that scales simple-to-complex, and was frustrated Claude wrote files before the design was finalized, requesting a handover summary for his second brain.

actions.text: Create handover conversation summarizing start.sh alias decisions for the second brain

essence: David sketched the start.sh aliasing scheme — an 's' alias checked against the jump-folder namespace, numbered s1/s2 (t1/t2 for test harnesses), run-plus-help for single-command dirs and help menu for multi-command dirs — and flagged it must be documented centrally in the second brain.

actions.text: Document the start.sh alias and numbering system centrally in the second brain

essence: David scoped the recurring pain of remembering per-project startup commands (npm/bun/docker across FliGen, FliDeck, FliHub, Storyline, SupportSignal) and asked for a plan — not implementation — for a consistent start.sh/'go' command that echoes commands while running them, without altering his jump system, wary of heavy third-party tools.

actions.text: Produce a plan of action (not implementation) for a unified start.sh/go startup command

essence: David debugged his Thai Magic Keyboard numpad emitting Unicode characters instead of digits in iTerm/Claude Code, seeking a programmatic macOS fix (numlock/fn-clear unclear on Thai layout), while mid-work on the POEM framework refining prompts with the Penny prompt-engineer agent.

Chiang Mai AI meetup deep-dive: looping agents with stop hooks in skills/sub-agents, multi-agent bug-finding competitions with false-positive penalties, message-passing via files/SQLite/git worktrees, swarm (self-organizing) vs mere background agents, human-on-the-loop until ~90% success, no-compact context engineering, double-sharding with findable headers over naive RAG, and Ralph-Wiggum mock loops (David's 20-screen UX iteration with a green/orange/red matrix).

Saturday Chiang Mai AI meetup (new venue): David demoed his day-old Omi wearable (open-source hardware/firmware, SDK/API, bought from Ian who brought units in), group covered Claude Code updates (skills fork-context, stop hooks baked into skills) and Ralph Wiggum loops for overnight builds/migrations; David asked for research on OMI APIs to send transcripts into Ian's Kybernesis, and recapped his BMAD chat with Brian (idea contributor, QuickFlow, vibe-to-BMAD hooks).

actions.text: Research OMI SDK/APIs and how to send transcripts to Kybernesis

essence: Two-hour teach-Voz-to-fish session: David configured Voz's (Vasilios, 70s, Windows) machine — WSL over PowerShell, Claude Desktop reinstall, git pull of the v-voz repo, GitHub collaborator access — and wired Claude background-agent-generated docs (git workflow, project structure, storyline JSON, cross-platform sync, storytelling techniques, starter second brain) into an interactive starter-question guide so Voz can work Languish the Artist / Boy and the Baker independently.

actions.text: Voz to explore the generated docs via Claude starter questions before reconnecting next week

essence: Prepping for the Voz session, David questioned why the repo wasn't using SSH and asked whether the new Languish the Artist video project folder should follow the Boy and the Baker / The Point starter structure, where video-project folder conventions are canonically documented, and requested a simple setup plus a README showing the full expected structure.

actions.text: Set up the video project folder with starter files and a README documenting the full expected structure

essence: David's message to Brian/BMAD team proposing a thin Zapier-style integration layer ('b-integrator'/'b-connect', modelled on Ian's 3-hour vibe-coded Claudiaire and its self-cloned Clotion) connecting enterprise Kanban tools (Jira/Asana/Linear/Notion) to agent harnesses, plus 'BVibe' — a vibe-coding front end to BMAD that silently accrues epics/stories/PRDs for a one-button brownfield upgrade once complexity bites.

David opened his contribution message to Brian and the BMAD Method Corp team (having just joined their core group), offering his two cents on three topics: the existing flow system, QuickFlow/Kanban/Autoclawed positioning, and turning BMAD conversation knowledge into visual representations.
